name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value exceeding the original input

name: align_to_one_not_identity
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_to_one_not_identity(a: usize)
    requires
        true,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning to 1 must be the identity since every integer is a multiple of 1

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The whole purpose of align_to_usize is to produce an aligned result; failing this breaks its contract

name: aligned_implies_changed
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_implies_changed(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: An already-aligned address must be a fixed point of align_to_usize; changing it would corrupt mappings

name: zero_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_zero_not_aligned(b: nat)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0 as nat, b),
{
}
reason: Zero is divisible by every positive integer so it must always be considered aligned
